purpose: Defines a structured learning path for one stack area in the KT vault. Each Course is one stack row.

course_id.description: Unique atomic identifier per saga numbering convention.

title.description: Short human-readable name. Should be specific to the outcome, not the technology alone.
title.example: Spring Boot 3 BFF with OAuth 2.0 PKCE

stack_area.description: Which row of the tech stack matrix this Course targets.

current_rating.description: Self-assessed proficiency before starting. 1 = cold, 5 = lead-ready.

outcome.description: One sentence stating what is operationally possible at the end that was not possible at the start. Behavior-based, not knowledge-based.
outcome.example: Can scaffold and run a Spring Boot 3 BFF with Google OAuth 2.0 PKCE in under 30 minutes from a clean machine.

prerequisites.description: Other course_ids or external skills that must be in place first.

modules.description: Ordered list of sittings. Each module is one focused study session.

title.description: Action-phrased; what gets done in this sitting.

hands_on_artifact.description: Concrete deliverable produced by the end of this module. Code file, config snippet, written summary, working demo.

verification.description: How you confirm the module landed. A passing test, a screenshot, a working endpoint, a written one-paragraph summary in your own voice.

duration_hours_total.description: Sum of module durations. Sanity-check against your weekly available study hours.

target_completion_date.description: Hard deadline. If duration exceeds available time, scope down or extend.

linked_project_id.description: The Project this Course feeds into. The Project is where Course knowledge gets exercised on real code.

notes.description: Free-form. Open questions, links to forum posts, things to come back to.

transitions.in_progress_to_blocked: External dependency (paid course not yet purchased, prerequisite skill missing, hardware not available). Add reason in notes.
transitions.in_progress_to_complete: All modules complete, all verifications pass. Update current_rating to target_rating.
transitions.any_to_abandoned: Course no longer relevant or replaced by a better course. Add reason in notes; never delete the record.

storage.rationale: Lives in 05_Wiki because Courses are durable knowledge. One file per Course makes them greppable and versionable in Git.

example_filled.title: Spring Boot 3 BFF with OAuth 2.0 PKCE
example_filled.outcome: Can scaffold a Spring Boot 3 BFF that authenticates a user via Google OAuth 2.0 with PKCE, issues an HttpOnly session cookie to the SPA, and proxies authenticated calls to a downstream resource server in under 30 minutes from a clean machine.

modules.title: Scaffold and run a minimal Spring Boot 3 web app
topics: Maven vs Gradle for this project
modules.hands_on_artifact: A running Spring Boot 3 app exposing GET /hello returning 200.
modules.verification: curl localhost:8080/hello returns the expected string. Screenshot in module log.

modules.title: Add Spring Security 6 with form login as a baseline
modules.hands_on_artifact: Same app now requires login before /hello returns 200.
modules.verification: anonymous request returns 302 to /login; authenticated request returns 200.

modules.title: Replace form login with OAuth 2.0 client (Google)
modules.hands_on_artifact: App redirects unauthenticated users to Google; on return, /hello shows the authenticated user's email.
modules.verification: browser flow works end-to-end. Capture the redirect chain in a written summary.

modules.title: Enable PKCE explicitly
modules.hands_on_artifact: PKCE-enabled config; observable code_challenge in the authorization request.
modules.verification: browser dev tools show code_challenge and code_challenge_method=S256 in the authorize URL.

modules.title: Issue session cookie to a sample SPA caller
topics: a tiny static index.html as the SPA stand-in
modules.hands_on_artifact: Static HTML page calling /api/me that returns the authenticated user.
modules.verification: fetch from the static page works; cookie visible in dev tools as HttpOnly.

modules.title: Proxy a downstream call through the BFF
modules.hands_on_artifact: BFF endpoint /api/proxy/data calls a stub resource server and returns its JSON to the SPA.
modules.verification: end-to-end: SPA calls BFF, BFF calls downstream with the right Authorization header, response reaches the SPA.

example_filled.notes: M04 PKCE module may already be covered automatically by Spring Security 6 defaults; verify whether explicit config is still required or if it is implicit for public clients.
